questions.question: What is a binary tree?
options: A) A tree with only one node
B) A tree with a fixed number of nodes
C) A tree where each node has at most two children
D) A tree with no children
questions.explanation: A binary tree is a tree where each node has at most two children (left child and right child).

questions.question: What is the purpose of the root node in a binary tree?
options: A) To store data
B) To represent the left subtree
C) To represent the right subtree
D) To serve as the entry point for traversals
questions.explanation: The root node serves as the entry point for traversals and represents the top-most node in the tree.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a left child and a right child in a binary tree?
options: A) Left child is always smaller than right child
B) Right child is always smaller than left child
C) Left child is always at the same depth as right child
D) Left and right children can be at different depths
questions.explanation: In a binary tree, left children are typically at a lower depth than right children.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the left and right child pointers in a binary tree?
options: A) To store data
B) To represent the parent node
C) To represent the left and right subtrees
D) To serve as the entry point for traversals
questions.explanation: The left and right child pointers represent the left and right subtrees of a node.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the height of a binary tree?
options: A) To measure the number of nodes in the tree
B) To measure the maximum distance from the root to a leaf node
C) To measure the minimum distance from the root to a leaf node
D) To measure the number of edges in the tree
questions.explanation: The height of a binary tree measures the maximum distance from the root to a leaf node.
